name: CCI Williams Double-Extreme Mean Reversion
edge_hypothesis: CCI and Williams %R have NEVER appeared in any ACCEPT strategy. Both measure price extremes from different angles — CCI measures deviation from statistical mean, Williams %R measures current close relative to the high-low range. When both simultaneously signal exhaustion, the convergent extreme indicates high-probability mean-reversion. An ADX < 25 gate restricts entries to ranging conditions where mean-reversion is reliable (ranging produces 19/23 ACCEPTs). This is a pure mean-reversion play using untested oscillators in the dominant profit regime.

variants.name: cci_willr_extreme_1h_8to1
variants.edge_hypothesis: CCI < -100 and WILLR < -80 together indicate deep oversold. ADX < 25 ensures ranging market where mean-reversion works. 8:1 R:R is the proven floor for ACCEPTs. 3 conditions keeps it tight but all are achievable — CCI regularly hits -100 and WILLR regularly hits -80 in ranging ETH.

variants.name: cci_willr_extreme_4h_10to1
variants.edge_hypothesis: 4h double-extreme is rarer but higher conviction. Wider 2.0x stop for 4h noise, 10:1 R:R. 4h CCI/WILLR extremes should produce cleaner mean-reversion entries with larger expected moves.

variants.name: cci_willr_extreme_1h_12to1
variants.edge_hypothesis: 12:1 R:R ceiling test on the same signal. RSI slingshot proved 12:1 can ACCEPT (PF=1.270). If CCI/WILLR double-extreme is a higher quality signal than RSI alone, 12:1 should capture the full mean-reversion swing. Fewer winners but each should be a large reversal move.
